courseName: Diploma in Emergency Medical Care (DIP EMC)
admissionRequirement: National Senior Certificate with Diploma endorsement, minimum NSC level 3 in each listed subject (18 points total). Older-system applicants (Senior Certificate/National Certificate) have separate equivalent thresholds — contact the School of Medicine to confirm. Applicants must pass Medical and Physical Fitness Evaluations. Leads to registration with the HPCSA as a Paramedic.

courseName: Higher Certificate in Emergency Medical Care (HCERT EMC)
admissionRequirement: National Senior Certificate with Higher Certificate endorsement, minimum NSC level 3 in English, Life and/or Physical Sciences, and two other subjects, OR level 4 in Mathematical Literacy in place of Mathematics (15 points total). Applicants must pass Medical and Physical Fitness Evaluations. Leads to registration with the HPCSA as an Emergency Care Assistant.

Mathematics, Physical Sciences, Life Sciences and English each require a minimum NSC level 4 (16 points combined); the remaining subjects (including Life Orientation) make up the rest of the 25-point APS total. Maximum duration: 6 years. Clinical training is at accredited hospitals in Gauteng, Limpopo and North West.
